lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Welcome to my court. I am King Arthur of the Knights of the Round Table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to become a Knight of the Round Table!

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Well then you must complete your quest to rescue Merlin. Talk to my knights if you need any help.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Welcome to my court. I am King Arthur of the Knights of the Round Table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to become a Knight of the Round Table!

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Really? Well then you will need to go on a quest to prove yourself worthy. My knights all appreciate a good quest.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Unfortunately, our current quest is to rescue Merlin. He recently got himself trapped in some sort of magical crystal. We've moved him from the cave we found him in and now he's upstairs in his tower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will see what I can do then.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Talk to my knights if you need any help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may come back and try that later.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Be sure that you come speak to me soon then.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Merlin's in a crystal. Little help?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: That is what we were hoping for from you, adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Well, ok, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: Good morrow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on getting Merlin out of that crystal?

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: Unfortunately not, Sirrah.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: The only person I know of who would possibly have been of help is Merlin...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, that's not actually too helpful.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: I am well aware of that, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: Greetings! I am Sir Lancelot, the greatest Knight in the land! What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to get Merlin out of the crystal.

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: Well, if the Knights of the Round Table can't manage it, I can't see how a commoner like you could succeed where we have failed.

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: Hello there adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any suggestions on freeing Merlin?

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: I've tried all the weapons I can find, yet none are powerful enough to break his crystal prison...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excalibur eh? Where would I find that?

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: The Lady of the Lake is looking after it I believe... but I know not where she resides currently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'll try and find someone who does.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: Hello there adventurer, what do you want of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like some advice on breaking that Crystal Merlin's trapped in.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: Sorry, I cannot help you with that.

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: Greetings to the court of King Arthur!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any suggestions on freeing Merlin?

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: My best guess would be some sort of magic. Unfortunately Merlin is our magic expert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, well, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Hail Arthur, King of the Britons!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know much about breaking magical crystals?

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Funnily enough...absolutely nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: Good day to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on how to get Merlin out of that crystal?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: I'm a little stumped myself. We've tried opening it with anything and everything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know how Merlin got trapped?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: I would guess this is the work of the evil Morgan Le Faye!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And where could I find her?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: She lives in her stronghold to the south of here, guarded by some renegade knights led by Sir Mordred.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea how to get into Morgan Le Faye's stronghold?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: No, you've got me stumped there...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for the information.

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: It is the least I can do.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea how to break into Mordred's fort do you?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: I am afraid not. Would that we could! Mordred and his cronies have been thorns in our side for far too long already!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. Thanks. See you later!

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Take care adventurer, Mordred is an evil and powerful foe.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: Good morrow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on getting into Mordred's fort?

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: Mordred... So you think he may be involved with the curse upon Merlin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good a guess as any right?

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: I think that you may be onto something there. Unfortunately his fortress is impregnable!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...I'll figure something out.

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: Hello there adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get into Mordred's Fortress.

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: So... you think Mordred's behind this? I'm afraid I don't have any suggestions...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'll try and find someone who does.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: Hello there adventurer, what do you want of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like some advice on breaking into Mordred's fort.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: Sorry, I cannot help you with that.

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: Greetings to the court of King Arthur!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any suggestions on getting into Mordred's fort?

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: My best guess would be using magic. Unfortunately Merlin is our magic expert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, well, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Hail Arthur, King of the Britons!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Hello. I need to get into Mordred's fort...

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Good luck with that!

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: Greetings! I am Sir Lancelot, the greatest Knight in the land! What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on how to get into Morgan Le Faye's stronghold?

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: That stronghold is built in a strong defensive position.

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: It's on a big rock sticking out into the sea.

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: There are two ways in that I know of, the large heavy front doors, and the sea entrance, only penetrable by boat. They get all their deliveries by boat from Catherby.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello! Would you like to trade?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that your ship?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Yes, I use it to make deliveries to my customers up and down the coast. These crates here are all ready for my next trip.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you deliver to the fort just down the coast?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Yes, I do have orders to deliver there from time to time. I think I may have some bits and pieces for them when I leave here next actually.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you drop me off on the way down please?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I don't think Sir Mordred would like that. He wants as few outsiders visiting as possible. I wouldn't want to lose his business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you worried about supplying evil knights?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hey, you gotta take business where you can find it these days! Besides, if I didn't supply them, someone else would.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do you deliver to?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Oh, various places up and down the coast. Mostly Karamja and Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose I could get a lift anywhere?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Sorry pal, but I'm afraid I'm not quite ready to sail yet.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I'm waiting on a big delivery of candles which I need to deliver further along the coast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, good luck with your business.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Thanks buddy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you rich then?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Business is going reasonably well... I wouldn't say I was the richest of merchants ever, but I'm doing fairly well all things considered.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hey buddy! Get away from my ship alright?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... uh... sorry...

lines.speaker: Sir Mordred
lines.text: You DARE to invade MY stronghold?!?! Have at thee knave!!!

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: STOP! Please... spare my son.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me how to untrap Merlin and I might.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: You have guessed correctly that I'm responsible for that.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: I suppose I can live with that fool Merlin being loose for the sake of my son.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: Setting him free won't be easy though.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: You will need to find a magic symbol as close to the crystal as you can find.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: You will then need to drop some bats' bones on the magic symbol while holding a lit black candle.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: This will summon a mighty spirit named Thrantax.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: You will need to bind him with magic words.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: Then you will need the sword Excalibur with which the spell was bound in order to shatter the crystal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where can I find Excalibur?

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: The lady of the lake has it. I don't know if she'll give it to you though, she can be rather temperamental.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I will go do all that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are the magic words?

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: You will find the magic words at the base of one of the chaos altars.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: Which chaos altar I cannot remember.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. He deserves to die.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK then.

lines.speaker: Candle maker
lines.text: Hi! Would you be interested in some of my fine candles?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got any black candles?

lines.speaker: Candle maker
lines.text: BLACK candles???

lines.speaker: Candle maker
lines.text: Hmmm. In the candle making trade, we have a tradition that it's very bad luck to make black candles.

lines.speaker: Candle maker
lines.text: VERY bad luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will pay good money for one...

lines.speaker: Candle maker
lines.text: I still dunno...

lines.speaker: Candle maker
lines.text: IF you can bring me a bucket FULL of wax.

lines.speaker: Candle maker
lines.text: Have you got any wax yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have some now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, not yet.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Know anything about Excalibur?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Um... it's a really good sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Know where I can find it?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Nope, sorry.

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: Good day to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on finding Excalibur?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: Unfortunately not, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: Good morrow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on finding Excalibur?

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: Unfortunately not, Sirrah.

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: Hello there adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to find Excalibur.

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any suggestions...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'll try and find someone who does.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: Hello there adventurer, what do you want of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like some advice on finding Excalibur.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: Sorry, I cannot help you with that.

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: Greetings to the court of King Arthur!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any suggestions on finding Excalibur?

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: My best guess would be some sort of spell. Unfortunately Merlin is our magic expert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, well, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Hail Arthur, King of the Britons!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to find Excalibur...

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Good luck with that!

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: Good day to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek the sword Excalibur.

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: Aye, I have that artefact in my possession.

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: 'Tis very valuable, and not an artefact to be given away lightly.

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: I would want to give it away only to one who is worthy and good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how am I meant to prove that?

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: I shall set a test for you.

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: First I need you to travel to Port Sarim. Then go to the upstairs room of the jeweller's shop there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. That seems easy enough.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the jewellery shop

lines.speaker: Beggar
lines.text: Please, stranger... my family and I are starving...

lines.speaker: Beggar
lines.text: Could you find it in your heart to spare me a simple loaf of bread?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, certainly.

lines.speaker: Beggar
lines.text: Thank you very much!

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: Well done. You have passed my test.

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: Here is Excalibur, guard it well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, certainly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... except I don't have any bread on me at the moment...

lines.speaker: Beggar
lines.text: Well, if you get some, you know where to come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't have any bread with me.

lines.speaker: Beggar
lines.text: Well, if you get some, you know where to come.

sections.title: Talking to the Beggar again if they were not given bread initially

lines.speaker: Beggar
lines.text: Have you got any bread for me yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, here you go.

lines.speaker: Beggar
lines.text: Thank you very much!

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: Well done. You have passed my test.

lines.speaker: The Lady of the Lake
lines.text: Here is Excalibur, guard it well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm wrong. I still don't have any bread. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, no, I still have none.

sections.title: Searching the altar for the spell
Dropping bat bones on the ritual circle with a lit black candle without having read the incantation
Dropping bat bones on the ritual circle without a lit black candle
Dropping bat bones on the ritual circle with a lit black candle after reading the incantation

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now what were those magic words again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Snarthtrick...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Candato...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Termon!

lines.speaker: Thrantax the Mighty
lines.text: GRAAAAAARGH!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Snarthanto...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Candon...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Termtrick!

lines.speaker: Thrantax the Mighty
lines.text: GRAAAAAARGH!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Snarthon...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Candtrick...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Termanto!

lines.speaker: Thrantax the Mighty
lines.text: GRAAAAAARGH!

lines.speaker: Thrantax the Mighty
lines.text: Thou hast me in thine control. So that I mayst return from whence I came, I must grant thee a boon. What dost thou wish of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wish to free Merlin from his giant crystal!

lines.speaker: Thrantax the Mighty
lines.text: GRAAAAAARGH!

lines.speaker: Thrantax the Mighty
lines.text: The deed is done. Thou mayst now shatter Merlin's crystal with Excalibur, and I can once more rest. Begone! And leave me once more in peace.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Thank you! Thank you! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: It's not fun being trapped in a giant crystal!

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Go speak to King Arthur, I'm sure he'll reward you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have freed Merlin from his crystal!

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Ah. A good job, well done. I dub thee a Knight Of The Round Table. You are now an honorary knight.
